vendor_context: Anker competitor (charging accessories, cables, screen protectors, smart home, wireless audio)
status: ACTIVE — Established global DTC + retail brand; Foxconn subsidiary since 2018

networks.name: Impact (Impact Radius)
networks.commission_rate: ~0.48–3.84% (affi.io/Impact data 2026-04-24); Shopper.com still shows 'up to 4%' — may lag
networks.commission_rate_us: 0.48% base (affi.io 2026-04-24, down from 0.8% recorded 2026-04-17); top end compressed to 3.84% from ~4%. Base rate dropped ~40%.
networks.notes: Primary global network. Program managed directly via belkin.com/company/affiliates. Payment via check, ACH, wire, or PayPal. $50 minimum payout threshold. Payment on 1st or 15th of month, or custom threshold. Daily reporting dashboard. Dynamic banners + HTML tag generator provided.

networks.name: CueLinks
networks.notes: Aggregator listing (June 2025 indexed). Lower than direct rate — standard aggregator haircut.

affiliate_program.commission_summary: ~0.48–3.84% on sales via Impact (as of 2026-04-24, down from ~0.8–4%); 2.7% via CueLinks aggregator. Rate appears category/market-tiered. Base rate compression detected. AU, DE, GB markets also active.
affiliate_program.aov_note: Low-AOV category ($20–$80 typical). Charging cables, screen protectors, wireless chargers. High volume, low per-sale payout.

company_status.financial: STABLE — Foxconn (Hon Hai Precision) subsidiary since 2018; privately held under parent
company_status.positioning: Premium accessory brand; Apple ecosystem alignment (MFi, MagSafe, AirPods accessories); global retail + DTC footprint. Competes with Anker on charging, cables, screen protectors.

competitive_intel.commission_vs_anker: Belkin ~1–4% via Impact vs Anker Amazon Associates ~1–4%. Both are structurally similar: low-AOV consumer electronics relying on managed affiliate networks. Neither has a standout affiliate advantage here.
competitive_intel.cookie_vs_anker: Belkin 30-day cookie via Impact. Anker primarily via Amazon (24-hour cookie). Belkin wins on attribution window — relevant for comparison-shopping content publishers.
competitive_intel.brand_positioning: Belkin competes on Apple ecosystem alignment and premium pricing; Anker competes on value and broad compatibility. Both court tech accessory review sites and YouTube channels. Belkin has stronger retail (Apple Store, Best Buy) but Anker dominates Amazon mindshare.
competitive_intel.affiliate_risk: LOW — Stable program, 40+ years in business, Foxconn parent backing
competitive_intel.aov_weakness: Both Belkin and Anker suffer from low AOV ($20–$80 range). Content affiliates earn fractions per sale — cashback and loyalty sites may be more productive than content for both brands.
competitive_intel.opportunity_for_anker: Anker should differentiate on AOV by pushing affiliates toward higher-ticket SKUs (SOLIX, projectors, robot vacuums) vs Belkin's cable/screen-protector core. Anker's broader product lineup (power stations, smart home) creates more affiliate content angles than Belkin's narrower accessory focus.

history.event: First audit. No prior file. Confirmed Impact (Impact Radius) as primary network. US commission ~0.8–4% (tiered/category-dependent). 30-day cookie. $50 payout minimum. AU, DE, GB also active. CueLinks aggregator listing at 2.7%. Company stable under Foxconn. Belkin and Anker structurally similar in affiliate economics — both low-AOV, Impact-adjacent programs.

Rate compression detected. affi.io (Impact Radius data) now shows 0.48%–3.84%, down from 0.8%–4% recorded 2026-04-17. Base rate dropped ~40%. Shopper.com still shows 'up to 4%' — may lag. Belkin affiliate landing page headline updated to 'iPhone 17 Chargers' — active push into Apple iPhone 17 accessory category. Discord alert fired.
